learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate an understanding of a philosophical and ethical foundation for choral leadership

demonstrate an understanding of basic choral conducting techniques including beat patterns, cueing, fermatas and release gestures; basic gestures of interpretation including dynamics and gradations, and articulation.
demonstrate an understanding of effective rehearsal technique devoting particular attention to vocal health and development
demonstrate an understanding of effective leadership skills while conducting the ensemble, communicating the music both verbally and gesturally
self-assess personal conducting style and technique through use of video
demonstrate a thorough knowledge of selected repertoire
demonstrate an understanding of the criteria for choosing good and approprate choral music
demonstrate an understanding of historical style as appropriate to choral music

assessments.due_date:
28/08/2026 5:00 pm

The task will be uploaded to Blackboard
assessments.task_description:
This initial conducting task gives you an opportunity to demonstrated the based elements of choral conducting gesture as developed in the initial weeks of the course.

You are to submit a video of yourself EITHER conducting a small group of singers OR a recording of the Renaissance madrigal April is in my mistress' face by Thomas Morley. If the latter, be sure to choose a recording that is somewhat stylistically appropriate for the genre and style.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Students will complete a 90 minute written test on aspects of Musical Style and Genre as is relevant to choral training. This will include two written tasks. The material for this test will derive directly from lectures and your extended reading.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will be at the end of the test time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible. In exceptional circumstances students may sit an alternative test.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

In-class listening test. No outside materials permitted.
assessments.task_description:
Due: Monday 12 October

Weighting:  30%

Submission Format: In class test


Students will complete a 60-minute listening test on aspects of Musical Style and Genre as relevant to choral training. This will include recognising known and unknown musical examples by title, composer, genre and style. The material for this test will derive directly from the listening list provided in week one.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission is at the end of the in-class test.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: Students will be assessed in Exam week on Monday, 9th and Tuesday, 10th November.
assessments.task_description:
Ensemble Leadership

Task Name: Rehearsal Conducting Demonstration

Due: Monday 9th November and Tuesday 10th November

Length: 10 minutes per student

Submission Format: Class group conducting + annotated score and rehearsal plan submitted via Blackboard

Weighting: 30%


In this final practical assessment, you will conduct a rehearsal of a designated choral work. This task is designed to assess your ability to lead a choral ensemble with technical clarity, musical responsiveness, and appropriate rehearsal strategies.

Each student must attend BOTH assessment days in order for the course to be completed.


Each student must prepare 3 set works and will be informed which piece to rehearse on the day of the test.

You are responsible for:

  • Preparing the rehearsal, including a written rehearsal plan
  • Conducting from an original score (a copy must be annotated)
  • Rehearsing the designated work with your peers

Assessment Focus

You will be assessed on your ability to:

  • Use beat patterns and/or conducting gestures appropriate to the chosen work
  • Demonstrate accurate cues and cut-offs, including effective use of fermatas and expressive releases
  • Demonstrate a strong musical understanding of the score through your conducting and rehearsal strategies
  • Respond in real-time to the sound of the ensemble, adjusting your approach to address musical needs
  • Execute a structured, goal-oriented rehearsal plan with effective pacing and clear outcomes

Required Materials

Submit the following on Blackboard prior to your tutorial:

  • A conductor’s score annotated with cues, phrasing, and interpretive details
  • A brief rehearsal plan outlining the musical goals, technical challenges, and strategies for rehearsal

Important Notes
  • You must conduct from a legally purchased copy of the score
  • The rehearsal should focus on shaping musical outcomes, not simply running the piece
  • You must ensure your preparation allows you to demonstrate a range of conducting and rehearsal skills
  • You may rehearse only an excerpt or section of the piece, depending on complexity and length


This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of the assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to attend both days of the assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submissions are in person and via Blackboard for the rehearsal plan and conductors' score. Instructions list as part of task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Conducting and interaction in class and ensemble

Weekly tasks are assigned, involving conducting gesture, and developing experiences of choral repertoire, rehearsal techniques and associated knowledge. Participation in 3160 choral ensemble.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09
